items.preText: Players are in the middle. England have a huddle. Still overcast. James Anderson, who loves this ground, takes the first over from the Radcliffe Road End. He needs one wicket for 300 at home. 10.55am We are almost ready to go. South Africa will be wearing black armbands in memory of Russell Domingo's mother who passed away earlier this week. Simon: "Reading that article on the pay dispute between the players and the Australian cricket board, it seems Australian cricket is in a real mess at the moment. If they don't resolve it, it may mean that they don't play any test cricket at all until it's resolved? Not sure I understand how it all works." There are, in effect, no players to make a team at the moment. 10.45am Johann: "Can the Cricinfo stats team please tell us the fate of teams batting first at Trent Bridge the last ten years or so?" Here you go Luke: "No surprise to see Duminy dropped. He is not a test standard number 4. Let's hope England keep up their good record in Test matches in the Midlands. They have not lost at Edgbaston or Trent Bridge since 2008." Ben: "That is a massive mistake by Faf. England's top order is already under pressure, there's nothing they would like less than facing Morkel, Philander and a (keen to impress, probably adrenaline heavy) Olivier. Now all the pressure is on South Africa. Can see why he might want to make a statement of intent but really, that's an error." Alasdair: "SA balance - 6 batter; 5 bowlers is correct. You need to bowl out the opposition to win. " Robert Brown: "Might be talked about a lot, but i feel there will be no ashes cricket this year, Australian cricket officials have walked away from negotiating now, the cricket ball in now in the players half of the pitch, and sad situation, both sides of the story have Dollars in their eyesight, and IPL , Big Bash or 20/20 cricket changed cricket forever ,plus they play with hunks of wood now , gone are the Bradman and Sobers Days" Here's our full coverage of the pay dispute. 10.40am Away from Trent Bridge for a moment, the "Universe boss" Chris Gayle is live on our Facebook page. Join the conversation here. Fidlerrr: "You've got to love this time of year in England... Tests during the day, The Blast in the evening! Cracker of a game last night!" Middlesex's collapse made it tight! 10.35am Doc: "Sitting in Lubumbashi, waiting on a delayed flight. This test, and your commentary, will save my sanity! SA's batting struggled at Lords, as did their fielding. Go figure, they strengthen their bowling." Barry: "Strange (wrong) decision by Faf, they pick an extra bowler and then decide to bat on a cloudy morning? Confused thinking... " Well, you could argue South Africa have a better balance to the side. Matt: "de Kock apparently batting at 4. Bold move, but potentially a very good one. Will be interesting to see how he goes." Yes, some suggestion of that. Will be interesting to see. 10.30am Toss time! South Africa won the toss and decide to bat. Faf du Plessis says it was a tricky decision with the cloud cover. Chris Morris comes into the XI at the expense of de Bruyn so a deeper bowling attack for South Africa. We knew about Duminy being dropped and, of course, Rabada misses out. Joe Root says he would have bowled, "so I'm pretty happy." Believes the balance of the England side is right for all conditions, but acknowledges they need a better start than they made in the first innings. James: "Morning everyone! I think George Dobell's analysis of the England team issues is excellent (go on - publish this and suck up to the boss!). In terms of the Ashes (and assuming Australia can put a team out), I have serious concerns about Ballance's ability against top class fast bowling attacks. His hundreds were against India, WI and Bangladesh. Dawson is essentially a defensive choice: Rashid (who did well in the Big Bash, and leg spinners tend to do OK in Aus) is surely a better bet. Think I have England favourites for this game, against a slightly depleted SA." Richard Dennis: "Duane Olivier averages 20.97 in first class cricket, not a bad replacement to bring in! It's a shame one of South Africa's four seamers isn't a little bit more handy with the bat, that would be a fearsome foursome." 10.25am Stiaan du Pless: "(not Faf's cousin or relative). I'm so glad that Faf's back. For me personally he is a better captain than AB. He brings calmness when needed. He is aggressive when needed. And he steps up, when needed. I think South Africa will thrash England in this test, and all to follow. Mark my words!! " Ashwin: "Its a 3 day weekend over here in Japan...no better way to cherish the holidays than to sit back and relish on what will be a series-defining comeback by the Proteas!" Konnichiwa 10.15am Moose: "Morning Gnasher, reckon England will be bowling this morning regardless of toss and we'll have torn through a lot of their batting by lunch. My previous predictions on here have been awful - is this one as bad?" We'll find out soon! Rob: "Don't listen to Nasser when it comes to the pitch analysis. I remember the Gabba in 2002." Poor Nasser! dez: "I actually agree with Moose! With their history at this ground & the overcast skies/green tinged wicket, surely England will bowl first should they win the toss, but i can also see SA opting to bat should they win it so same result!" 10.10am Dan: "Do we think the return of experienced Faf will offset the relative inexperience on Olivier and loss of Duminy? Looks like SA are finally starting to blood new players." One huge player back, one missing. Du Plessis has a huge influence. Kaushik: "Remember those days when I could easily spot a couple of seam bowling all rounders in the proteas side.I can't remember the if I ever saw South Africa struggled to field a balanced XI." Sounds as though Chris Morris has a good chance of making the XI Barney: "How is the pitch looking, is it a bat or bowl first wicket?" Nasser Hussain giving a pitch reports. Says there is 6mm of grass, was 7.5mm in the 2015 Test. He doesn't think it's a bad toss to lose. 10.00am Alan: "What's the weather like? Will it have a impact on the toss this morning?" Overcast and a bit breezy. But dry. Here's George Dobell from the ground: "Hello from TB (Trent Bridge, not tuberculosis). Not hugely optimistic about the weather. But I guess it's being so cheerful keeps me going." The forecast suggests it will get better during the day. One of the issues for South Africa at Lord's was no-balls. It cost them two wickets, one of them from Morne Morkel for the 13th time. He knows it's a record he would rather not have. 9.55am Rob: "Morning Gnasher. Hoping that England bowl first and we have a repeat of the game here in 2015." That was one of the more dramatic mornings! Mark: "Some pundits are already predicting 4-0 to England. I am reminded of the 2003 series when, after 2 Tests, you would have got very short odds on South Africa winning by at least 3-0: it ended 2-2. South Africa got just about everything wrong at Lord's and have been awful (with notable exceptions) in the 3 limited overs tournaments that they have played on this tour, but you would be a fool to write them off so quickly. This England side is nowhere near as good as it was made to look in the 1st Test." Lord's was also a different experience for this man - Alastair Cook was back in the ranks. Mel Farrell spoke to him. And for something a little different, Quinton de Kock went to the factory where his bats are made. 9.45am Hello everyone and welcome to our coverage of the second Test between England and South Africa from Trent Bridge. England took the honours comprehensively at Lord's with a 211-run victory. South Africa have their captain, Faf du Plessis, back and can he inspire a revival? I'm Andrew McGlashan and along with Alan Gardner will bring you all the action. England have confirmed an unchanged XI, but South Africa have one big selection call: dropping JP Duminy. Is that the end for a frustrating Test career?. They have also needed to replace Kagiso Rabada, who is suspended for this game, and it could take two players to do it. Duanne Olivier will come into the side and there's a late decision over Chris Morris will join him. While things could barely have gone better for Joe Root in his first Test as captain there are some lingering issues for England to work out. And a few of them revolve around the familiar problems in the top order.
items.text: full, down the leg side, Elgar manages to catch up with it and flicks down to long leg

items.preText: Three slips and a gully for Kuhn
items.text: that's a good one! Beats the edge with an outswinger that moves a long way. Pitches just outside off, made him play, and curves past the bat
more late movement, 81mph, on off stump and Kuhn gets squared up as this comes off an open face towards gully
a touch fuller, outside off, he can watch this swing and leave alone on the front foot
straighter, a full delivery, defended towards mid-on with a slightly angled bat and with a closed face. Dicey
full at the stumps, driven back to the bowler...an encouraging first over for Anderson

items.preText: Jackie: "I would honestly bat Philander ahead of Morris. Far better temperament for test cricket" South Africa will hope neither are needed to today. Here's Stuart '8 for 15' Broad
items.text: round the wicket, on the stumps, Elgar ends up almost square on as he defends
a fraction shorter, on off stump, 83mph, defended to the off side
items.postText: A short leg comes in place

items.text: fuller, at off stump, pushed back down the pitch
well bowled, angled in at off stump, straightens off the seam and takes a thick outside edge along the ground towards fourth slip
items.postText: Roddy: "Maybe I missed the discussion at the Lord's test, but it seems Jimmy didn't bowl as much as he could in the second innings. Is he fully fit since he broke down for Lancs a few weeks ago, or was it because he couldn't get the ball out of the spinners' hands?" More the latter. Spinners did the job

items.text: that's not his best ball, speared well down the leg side...re-calibrate that radar
full outside off, left alone
short of a length outside off, left alone
past the edge again! It's hooping for Jimmy. Good length outside off, beats the bat by a long way in the end. Maybe could be a fraction fuller
gets straighter, clipped off the pads to square leg. Kuhn may feel he missed out there
just short of a length at off stump, 81mph, defended with a little jump behind the line to cover
that's a good bit of a cricket, tabs a short-of-a-length ball to the off side and they see the chance for a quick single which gets him off the mark
adjusts to the left hander, 86mph, on off stump and defended
items.postText: Mark: "Any sign of any swing in the early exchanges?" Yep, nice shape for Anderson

items.text: short of a length outside off, a comfortable one to leave alone
that's a tighter leave! Nips back from a full length and zips past the off stump as Kuhn shoulders arms
chopped into the off side from the back foot, almost straight to Stokes at point who fumbles...a clean pick-up could have made that a tight run
round the wicket, takes his bottom hand off the bat as he defends a length ball to the leg side
fuller, outside off, he presses forward and plays towards point
a strangled appeal for caught behind as this jags back off the seam into Elgar, beats the inside edge and comes off the back leg through to Bairstow
items.postText: TJP: "I like this new combination. Would work even better with Rabada back, as he is slightly better with the bat than Olivier. Having Philander, Maharaj & Rabada at 8-10 covers for Morris at 7. People go on about SA batting at Lords - Conceding 450 in 1st innings cost us the match and Morris cant do any worse than JPD anyway. Agree with Jackie that Vernon should bat 7 and Morris 8 though!"

items.text: tight on off stump, 85mph, played to the on side
